compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
main's CI (`gerrit-verify`) is red on `tests/unit/test_import_cycles.py::test_committed_baseline_matches_current_tree`, blocking every change based on main. The import-cycle strangler guard produces a NON-DETERMINISTIC `largest_scc_size` under CI's random test order, and is being removed as too fragile to justify. This change also makes `test_execution_mode_dispatch` environment-independent.

## Reproduction Steps

1. On main (`6a28e9a5a`), run the default suite the way CI does — random order (no `-p no:randomly`): `pytest -m "not integration and not external"`.
2. Observe `test_committed_baseline_matches_current_tree` fail with `assert 36 == 35` — and on a later run `assert 37 == 35`. The number climbs run-to-run on byte-identical inputs (same commit, same grimp 3.15, same Python).
3. Run the same test in isolation OR with `-p no:randomly` (fixed order): it passes with the clean baseline (35).

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: an import-cycle guard, if kept, must be deterministic — the same source must yield the same result regardless of test order.
- Actual: the guard runs `grimp.build_graph("rebar")` IN-PROCESS inside the test, so an earlier test's in-memory package-path / `sys.modules` mutations leak into grimp's view and inflate the SCC count (35→36→37) depending on random test order. This red-ed main non-deterministically.

## Context

Discovered while landing the reconciler abort-isolation fix (Gerrit change 331): CI's `Verified-1` was NOT that change's fault — it was this pre-existing order-dependent guard flakiness on main. The `ModelHTTPError` ImportError seen locally is a separate, local-only artifact of the loopback-proxy bypass (commit 4b9e49a57) firing against the test's empty `pydantic_ai` stub; it never affected CI (no loopback there). Decision: the guard's fragility outweighs its benefit — remove it rather than add subprocess isolation. Bundle the small execution-mode test hardening in the same change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `scripts/check_import_cycles.py`, `tests/unit/test_import_cycles.py`, and
      `.import-cycle-baseline.json` are removed.
- [ ] The now-orphan `grimp` dependency (used only by the guard) is removed from
      `pyproject.toml`, along with the guard's per-file ruff ignore.
- [ ] The stale `scripts/check_import_cycles.py` reference in
      `src/rebar/llm/workflow/editor_server.py` is removed/reworded.
- [ ] `test_execution_mode_dispatch::test_single_turn_runner_builds_agent_with_no_tools`
      passes even when `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is a loopback host (stubs the bypass).
- [ ] The default suite passes on py3.12 in random order (no import-cycle failure).
- [ ] `make lint` passes; no other test or source references the removed files.

file_impact.reason: remove the fragile import-cycle guard script
drop the now-orphan grimp dep + the guard ruff ignore
stub the loopback bypass so the test is env-independent

compiled_state.title: main CI red: fragile import-cycle guard yields non-deterministic SCC under random test order
